name: Superstruct (Composable Data Validation)

agent_readiness.notes: Composable data validation with detailed error messages. assert(data, Type) throws StructError with path info. is(data, Type) returns boolean. validate(data, Type) returns [error, value] tuple. Built-in types: object(), string(), number(), boolean(), array(), union(), intersection(), literal(), optional(), nullable(). Coercion support. Custom types via struct(). Lighter than Zod (~5KB vs ~14KB). By Ian Storm Taylor (Slate editor creator). Good detailed error messages. Less ecosystem than Zod.

gotchas: TypeScript inference less ergonomic than Zod
best_when: Bundle-size-sensitive apps needing validation (~5KB vs Zod's ~14KB)
avoid_when: New projects (Zod has better ecosystem and TypeScript inference)
Need Zod/Valibot ecosystem integrations (react-hook-form, tRPC, etc.)
summary: Superstruct is a composable data validation library (~5KB). assert/is/validate functions with detailed StructError path info. Composable types: object, string, union, intersection, literal, optional. Custom types via struct(). Less ecosystem than Zod but lighter. Maintained but Zod has overtaken it as the ecosystem standard. Choose Zod for ecosystem integrations; Superstruct or Valibot for size-sensitive projects.
